titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Susan Stewart Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11299108.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 24, 2020 at 03:12

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, correspondence, journals, datebooks, notebooks, teaching materials, printed materials, awards, artwork, and audiovisual materials by, to, or relating to poet Susan Stewart.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 52 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 53 (email): Restricted until 2038. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Digital files (email correspondence): ‡a Restricted until 2038. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Susan Stewart Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.
Online access or distribution of paper correspondence prohibited until 2038; this material may not be photographed in the reading room.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into fourteen series: I. Correspondence. II. Writings. III. Writings of others. IV. Publishing papers. V. Teaching papers. VI. Fellowships, grants, and awards. VII. Conferences, seminars, scholarships, and lectures. VIII. Journals and notebooks. IX. Photographs. X. Travel papers. XI. Other papers. XII. Printed materials. XIII. Audiovisual materials. XIV. Computer media.

bioghist.head: Susan Stewart
bioghist.p: Susan Stewart (born 1952) is an American poet, literary critic, and translator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund and the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2013.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Susan Stewart Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

did.unittitle: Abrahams, Roger D. to Candinas, Pia
Wilner, Eleanor to Zwicky, Jan, unidentified correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stewart wrote "Garden Agon," [On Ian Hamilton Finlay's "Little Sparta"] published in Representations (1998, #62). Reprinted in Space, Site, Intervention: Situating Installation Art, edited Erika Suderburg (University of Minnesota Press, 2000), and Stewart's The Open Studio: Essays in Art and Aesthetics (University of Chicago, 2005) as "Garden Agon: Ian Hamilton Finlay's Little Sparta, 1993–97." In addition to research and manuscripts there is Stewart's correspondence with Finlay.

did.unittitle: Correspondence primarily regarding Garden Agon article
Research – Notes for Gardens and Wars

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including images of Susan Stewart "taken in fall 1999 in Alfred, New York, with Ann Hamilton and made by placing a pinhole camera in the mouth's cavity and opening the lips to expose the film to light." Photographs from this series were used for Stewart's Poetry and the Fate of the Senses.

did.unittitle: Assorted papers relatin to Ann Hamilton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 original prototypes, 1980s–2012, and 2 paper hand/masks "from the end of the year."

did.unittitle: "How to Read a Poem" – syllabus and readings
Manuscript – Divorce: Twelve Prologues to a Story / For Charles Baxter
Manuscript – Fairy Tale for a Whore
Manuscript – The Imaginary Peasant – Grant Proposal
Manuscript – A Wild Journey to Bedlam
Acoustical Prehistory of Poetry by Brunella Antomarini to Freedom from Imagination
Games From Children – The American Poetry Review to Love Lessons by Alda Merini – Translation by Susan Stewart
Making and Doing – Harryette Mullen (proof) to The Poem of the Mind
Poetry and Thinking Symposium Notes to Still Thoughts on Moving Images
Tacita Dean/Hugh Lane Gallery to 20th and 21st century Italian poems in translation, Assorted

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Susan Stewart. Including correspondence and drafts.

did.unittitle: The Open Studio: Essays on Art and Aesthetics
The Poet's Freedom: A Notebook on Making
The Poet's Freedom: A Notebook on Making - drafts
Poetry and the Fate of the Senses
Poetry and the Fate of the Senses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Susah Stewart and Sarah Teardo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems of Milo De Angelis. Edited and translated by Susan Stewart and Patrizio Ceccagnoli.

did.unittitle: The Elements - Flow from the Sacrifice of Water
The Elements - lithographs, drafts, page proofs, Josephine Miles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishing papers consist of correspondence, contracts, agreements, permissions, and other papers relating to the publication of Stewart's poetry and articles in books, journals, and other media. Also includes rejection letters, related clippings and ephemera, reviews, publicity, flyers, posters, and invitations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teaching papers consists of syllabi, notes, and other papers documenting Stewart's teaching work at universities including Columbia University, George Mason University, Johns Hopkins University, Princeton University, Stanford University, Temple University, University of California, Berkeley, and University of Pennsylvania.

did.unittitle: Drawing the Line in Poetry and the Visual Arts: two 26 x 40 pieces of paper used as material samples
American Academy of Arts and Science award certificate
Printed materials relating to works of others

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Restriction applies only to the email files on this drive, which are not to be consulted before January 1, 2038 (or Susan Stewart's death, should that be before January 1, 2038).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Printed email files: Not to be consulted before January 1, 2038 (or Susan Stewart's death, should that be before January 1, 2038) .
